rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [2+2] cycloaddition of two alpha,beta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds (chalcones) to give tetrasubstituted cyclobutanes using NaI as redox mediator in an undivided cell
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (2 I- -> I2) generates I2 which iodinates the enolate/enone; cathode reduces water to H2 and HO-, regenerating base/closing the loop in a paired-electrolysis scenario

key_intermediates.name: iodine (I2) generated at the anode from 2 I-
open-chain bis-iodo / Michael adduct 2a' (precursor to cyclobutane via intramolecular SN2/displacement of I-)

electron_flow: At the anode, 2 I- are oxidized (loss of 2 e-) to I2, which iodinates the chalcone enolate to give an alpha-iodo ketone; this intermediate undergoes Michael addition with a second chalcone enolate, and intramolecular displacement of iodide closes the cyclobutane ring. At the cathode, 2 H2O are reduced to H2 + 2 HO-, which regenerates the enolate base.
notes: Mechanism in Scheme 4 is presented as 'plausible' without explicit radical traps, KIE, or CV data on these pages. NaI acts as both supporting electrolyte and redox mediator (I-/I2 shuttle). The chalcone precursors 1 are prepared by classical Claisen-Schmidt condensation (NaOH, EtOH/H2O) prior to electrolysis. Reaction is run in an undivided cell with PGE anode and stainless-steel cathode at 20 mA, MeCN-MeOH (3:1), 15 h (11.19 F/mol).
